issues.text: 53. The March 31, 2026 guardianship petition seeks broad authority over Jane Kay Cortez, including residence-related circumstances, APS interface, and possible law-enforcement transport, while also stating that no prior guardian, trustee, health-care representative, or attorney-in-fact previously existed.

53. The March 31, 2026 guardianship petition seeks broad authority over Jane Kay Cortez, including residence-related circumstances, APS interface, and possible law-enforcement transport, while also stating that no prior guardian, trustee, health-care representative, or attorney-in-fact previously existed.

54. My earlier review of records in this matter repeatedly raised the possibility that prior authority-related proceedings or appointments may already have addressed overlapping control issues concerning Jane Kay Cortez.

55. I do not claim final issue preclusion has already been proven. I understand final collateral-estoppel adjudication requires proof of final decision, issue identity, party/privity, and full and fair opportunity, and that clerk-certified copies would strengthen that proof.

1. APS / police allegation omits same-day restraining-order context.
1. APS / police allegation omits same-day restraining-order context.
2. Benjamin did not just recently begin living with Jane; he has lived with her for more than six years.
3. The housing crisis included HACC / Quantum delay and Section 18 relocation failure, not just alleged Benjamin conduct.
4. The petition seeks coercive and overbroad relief: transport, placement, plenary authority, and fees from Jane's funds.

5. Trust / inheritance / authority papers were not cleanly produced even while Solomon sought broad control, and the earlier restraining-order exhibits appear to show Jane describing papers she signed with Sam / Solomon that she did not understand, including one she thought had something to do with her dad's will.
6. If the petition is materially barred or defect-ridden, the Court may also need to ask whether it should have been filed or maintained in this form.
1. deny further coercive writ-based relief;
1. deny further coercive writ-based relief;
2. deny plenary and placement authority on the present showing;
3. deny fees from Jane's funds;
4. set any further hearing only on genuine guardianship questions, with a clean notice and predicate record.
5. reserve any sanctions decision unless and until the Court decides whether the petition was materially barred or seriously defective.
